## Objective Requirements

- `OBJ-01`: The four governing design documents exist under docs/design and drive implementation.
- `OBJ-02`: TapDB acts as a semantic object, lineage, lifecycle, and provenance boundary rather than a hot-path store replacement.
- `OBJ-03`: Locations, spaces/rooms, sessions, events, devices, and personas are explicit and linkable domain objects.
- `OBJ-04`: Semantic memory has source evidence, candidate/commit state, recall explanation, and tombstone deletion semantics.
- `OBJ-05`: Recognition separates observation, hypothesis, consent-used, presence, and unknown-person behavior.
- `OBJ-06`: Devices authenticate as devices, report heartbeat/location state, and use the action lifecycle for command routing.
- `OBJ-07`: Actions record proposal, approval/policy, dispatch/execution/result, idempotency, and audit evidence.
- `OBJ-08`: Consent and privacy gates apply at use time, and audit/observability surfaces expose state and integrity checks.
- `OBJ-09`: GUI/API expose major V1 surfaces for memory, recognition, lineage, locations, sessions, observability, persona, and capabilities.
- `OBJ-10`: Persona is configurable per agent/session rather than a hardcoded-only production path.
- `OBJ-11`: Generated status maps objectives to repository evidence and supports an objective completion threshold.
- `OBJ-12`: At least one real deployed-stack smoke path and local/browser workflow path are represented without substituting local-only proof for deployed acceptance.
- `OBJ-13`: Tracked code and current design docs contain no production dummy recognizer path, stale TapDB copy, compatibility shim, or obsolete planning path.
